<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Trigger value change on target (this propagates notifications)
    target->valueChanged();

    // Check notification status on the observer
    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <mutex>
#include <set>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

namespace reaction {

class ThreadManager {
public:
    /**
     * Get the singleton instance.
     * @return ThreadManager& singleton reference.
     */
    [[nodiscard]] static ThreadManager &getInstance() noexcept {
        static ThreadManager instance;
        return instance;
    }

    /**
     * Register a new thread into the manager.
     * @param threadId The ID of the thread to register.
     */
    void registerThread(std::thread::id threadId) noexcept {
        ConditionalUniqueLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(m_threadsMutex);
        m_activeThreads.insert(threadId);
    }

    /**
     * Check if thread safety is currently enabled.
     * @return true if thread safety is enabled, false otherwise.
     */
    bool isThreadSafetyEnabled() const noexcept {
        return m_threadSafetyEnabled.load();
    }

    /**
     * Enable thread safety mode.
     * Once enabled, cannot be disabled during runtime.
     */
    void enableThreadSafety() noexcept {
        bool expected = false;
        if (m_threadSafetyEnabled.compare_exchange_strong(expected, true)) {
            // First thread to enable wins
        }
    }

    /**
     * Get the number of active threads for debugging/monitoring.
     * @return size_t Number of threads that have been registered.
     */
    size_t getThreadCount() const noexcept {
        ConditionalUniqueLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(m_threadsMutex);
        return m_activeThreads.size();
    }

private:
    /**
     * Generic atomic operation helper with locking.
     * @tparam F Operation function type (should take no args and return bool indicating if changed).
     * @param operation The operation to perform on the data.
     * @param alwaysChanged If true, always consider the operation as changing the value.
     */
    template <typename F, typename T>
    void atomicOperation(F &&operation, T alwaysChanged = false) noexcept {
        bool changed = false;
        {
            ConditionalUniqueLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(m_threadsMutex);
            try {
                changed = operation();
                if (alwaysChanged) {
                    changed = true;
                }
            } catch (...) {
                // Lock is automatically released on exception
                throw; // Re-throw the original exception
            }
        } // Lock is automatically released here
    }

    std::conditional_shared_mutex m_threadsMutex; ///< Conditional mutex for thread-safe access to active threads.
    std::set<std::thread::id> m_activeThreads;     ///< Set of active thread IDs tracked by this manager.

    /**
     * Thread safety mode flag.
     * Once enabled, cannot be disabled during runtime.
     */
    std::atomic<bool> m_threadSafetyEnabled{false};

    // Delete copy constructor and assignment operator to avoid copying
    ThreadManager(const ThreadManager &) = delete;
    ThreadManager &operator=(const ThreadManager &) = delete;

public:
    /**
     * Always returns false for thread manager (singleton).
     * @return false.
     */
    [[nodiscard]] bool isSingleThreaded() const noexcept {
        return false;
    }
};

} // namespace reaction

// Implementation of conditional mutex abstractions (usually in a separate file)
namespace reaction {

/**
 * RAII lock guard generator for conditional mutexes.
 * @param GuardName The name of the guard class
 * @param LockMethod The locking method name
 * @param UnlockMethod The unlocking method name
 */
#define REACTION_DEFINE_LOCK_GUARD(GuardName, LockMethod, UnlockMethod)         \
    template <typename Mutex>                                                   \
    class GuardName {                                                           \
    public:                                                                     \
        explicit GuardName(Mutex &mutex) noexcept(noexcept(mutex.LockMethod())) : m_mutex(mutex) { m_mutex.LockMethod(); } \
        ~GuardName() noexcept(noexcept(m_mutex.UnlockMethod())) { m_mutex.UnlockMethod(); } \
        GuardName(const GuardName &) = delete;                                \
        GuardName &operator=(const GuardName &) = delete;                     \
    private:                                                                    \
        Mutex &m_mutex;                                                         \
    }

// Generate conditional lock guards
REACTION_DEFINE_LOCK_GUARD(ConditionalLockGuard, lock, unlock);
REACTION_DEFINE_LOCK_GUARD(ConditionalSharedLockGuard, lock_shared, unlock_shared);

/**
 * Alias for conditional shared lock guard with better readability.
 */
template <typename Mutex>
using ConditionalSharedLock = ConditionalSharedLockGuard<Mutex>;

/**
 * Alias for conditional unique lock guard with better readability.
 */
template <typename Mutex>
using ConditionalUniqueLock = ConditionalLockGuard<Mutex>;

} // namespace reaction

// Cleanup macro to avoid pollution
#undef REACTION_DEFINE_LOCK_GUARD
} // namespace test_reaction
output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Tests thread manager registration and thread safety configuration.
 *
 * @param enableSafety If true, enables thread safety; otherwise, leaves it disabled.
 * @return true if thread safety is enabled after operation, false otherwise.
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // Enable thread safety if requested
    if (enableSafety) {
        threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    // Return current thread safety status
    return threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Add nodes to the graph (required before batch operations)
    graph.addObserverNode(node1);
    graph.addObserverNode(node2);

    // Step 4: Create a batch ID
    auto batchId = graph.createBatch();

    // Step 5: Register nodes to the batch
    graph.addNodeToActiveBatch(node1, batchId);
    graph.addNodeToActiveBatch(node2, batchId);

    // Step 6: Verify nodes are in the batch
    if (!graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || !graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 7: Unregister the batch
    graph.closeActiveBatch(batchId);

    // Step 8: Verify nodes are no longer in the batch
    if (graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) {
        return false;
    }

    // Success
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);

    // Get ObserverGraph instance and add observer relationship
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    // Notify target and check observer was notified
    target->valueChanged();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Record call count before reset
    int callCountBeforeReset = observer->getCallCount();

    // Reset observer
    observer->reset();

    // Notify target again and check observer wasn't notified
    target->valueChanged();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != callCountBeforeReset) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Existing TestObserverClosure class definition...

/**
 * Function: Test node closure functionality
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the observer node
 * @return bool Whether node closure worked correctly (relationships cleaned up)
 */
bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto source = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>("source");
    
    // Add observer relationship: source observes the created observer
    graph.addObserver(source, observer);

    // Notify the source node, which should trigger the observer
    source->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer was notified
    if (!observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer state
    observer->m_changed = false;
    observer->m_callCount = 0;

    // Close the observer node
    graph.closeObserver(observer);

    // Notify source again
    source->valueChanged();

    // Verify observer wasn't notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged() || observer->getCallCount() != 0) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

// Data model definitions (as provided)

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return true if transactional update prevented cycle and preserved state, false otherwise
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("targetNode");

    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(source);
    graph.addNode(target);

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship
    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Verify initial notification
    source->changedNoNotify();
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset nodes
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update creating cycle
    try {
        // Create observer edge from target to source (forms cycle)
        auto edge = std::make_shared<ObserverEdge>();
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(
            source,
            {
                {target, edge}  // Adding target as observer of source creates cycle
            }
        );
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Cycle detected - expected
    } catch (...) {
        // Unexpected exception
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Verify original relationship preserved
    source->changedNoNotify();
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 9: Check call counts (should be 2 after reset and second notification)
    if (source->getCallCount() != 2 || target->getCallCount() != 2) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(observer);
    }
    
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    // Register observers to the graph
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addNode(observer);
    }
    // Add observers to watch the target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    target->notify();
    
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // Reset observers to ensure clean state
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // Set up observer chain
    graph.addObserver(target, observer1);
    graph.addObserver(observer1, observer2);
    
    // Trigger notifications
    target->notify();
    
    // Calculate results
    int totalCalls = observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = (observer1->getCallCount() > 0) && (observer2->getCallCount() > 0);
    
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_expression(int a, int b) {
    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr::create(b);
    auto sum = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::add(varA, varB);
    auto expr = reaction::BinaryOpExpr::mul(sum, reaction::ValueExpr{2});
    return expr.eval().get<int>();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::createVar("varA", a);
    auto varB = reaction::createVar("varB", b);
    
    // Create first calculation (varA + varB)
    auto calc1 = reaction::createCalc("calc1", [this]() {
        return reaction::getVal(varA) + reaction::getVal(varB);
    }, {varA, varB});
    
    // Create second calculation (calc1 * 2)
    auto calc2 = reaction::createCalc("calc2", [this]() {
        return reaction::getVal(calc1) * 2;
    }, {calc1});
    
    // Update varA to newA, triggering dependency chain
    reaction::updateVar(varA, newA);
    
    // Return the final result of calc2
    return reaction::getVal(calc2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// PersonRecord implementation
PersonRecord::PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
    : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

const std::string& PersonRecord::getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
int PersonRecord::getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

void PersonRecord::setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
void PersonRecord::setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

// test_reactive_fields implementation
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    auto calc = reaction::make_calc(
        [this](const std::string& name, int age) {
            return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
        },
        person.m_name, person.m_age
    );

    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    return calc.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <mutex>
#include <set>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <vector>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                           int initialB,
                                           int nonBatchA,
                                           int nonBatchB,
                                           int batchA,
                                           int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables a and b
    reaction::VarExpr<int> a(initialA);
    reaction::VarExpr<int> b(initialB);

    // Create calculation c = a + b
    reaction::CalcExpr<int> c = a + b;

    // Get weak pointer to c's node to track notifications
    auto c_weak = c.getPtr();
    int initialCount = c_weak->getPtr()->m_notifyCount.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int countAfterNonBatch = c_weak->getPtr()->m_notifyCount.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

    // Execute batch updates
    {
        reaction::Batch batch([this](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
            // Update a and b within the batch
            a.set(batchA);
            b.set(batchB);
        });
        batch.execute();
    }
    int countAfterBatch = c_weak->getPtr()->m_notifyCount.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(c.get(), countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    reaction::Var<int> varA(valueA, "varA");
    reaction::Var<int> varB(valueB, "varB");
    
    // Create a calculation depending on varA and varB with a name
    reaction::Calc<int> calcAB([=]() {
        return varA.get() + varB.get();
    }, "calcAB");
    
    // Create an action depending on the calculation with a name
    reaction::Action actionCalc([=]() {
        // Action logic (no-op here)
        return reaction::Void{};
    }, calcAB, "actionCalc");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::ostringstream oss;
    oss << varA.getName() << ", " << varB.getName() << ", " 
        << calcAB.getName() << ", " << actionCalc.getName();
    
    return oss.str();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>
#include <atomic>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                     double firstUpdate,
                                                     double secondUpdate,
                                                     double diffThreshold,
                                                     double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive sensor node
    auto sensorNode = std::make_shared<reaction::Node<double>>();
    sensorNode->set(initialValue);

    // Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<std::string, double>>();
    calcExpr->addObCb(sensorNode->getPtr());
    calcExpr->setFun([levelThreshold](double val) {
        return val > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });

    // Create change-trigger with threshold
    auto filterTrig = std::make_shared<reaction::ChangeTrig<double>>(diffThreshold);
    calcExpr->setTrig(filterTrig);

    // Create status node for output
    auto statusNode = std::make_shared<reaction::Node<std::string>>();
    statusNode->setExpr(calcExpr);

    // Track recomputation counts
    int recomputeCount1 = 0;
    int recomputeCount2 = 0;

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensorNode->set(firstUpdate);
    {
        auto shared_this = calcExpr->shared_from_this();
        recomputeCount1 = shared_this->m_recomputeCount.load();
    }

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorNode->set(secondUpdate);
    {
        auto shared_this = calcExpr->shared_from_this();
        recomputeCount2 = shared_this->m_recomputeCount.load();
    }

    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusNode->getValue();

    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, recomputeCount1, recomputeCount2);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode methods
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    ++m_notificationCount;
}

inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const {
    return m_notificationCount;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields methods
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    ProfileFields profile{initialScore, false}; // Assuming initialStatus is false
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;

    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(ownerNode);

    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile.score(), ownerNode);
    fieldGraph.bindField(profile.active(), ownerNode);

    // Update score and record notifications
    {
        auto score = profile.score();
        score += scoreDelta;
        score.notify();
    }
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();

    // Update active and record notifications
    {
        auto active = profile.active();
        active = !active.get();
        active.notify();
    }
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();

    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScoreUpdate,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatusUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of RecordingNode::valueChanged and notificationCount
inline void RecordingNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) noexcept {
    ++m_notifications;
}

inline int RecordingNode::notificationCount() const noexcept {
    return m_notifications;
}

// Implementation of test_observer_state_rollback
RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    graph->addNode(node1);
    graph->addNode(node2);

    // Initial dependency: node2 observes node1
    graph->addObCb(node1, node2);

    // Save current state for rollback
    auto savePoint = graph->saveStateForRollback();

    // Replace dependencies transactionally
    {
        reaction::ObserverGraph::TransactionGuard g([this](const std::string& error) {
            // Rollback on failure
        });
        // Remove existing observers of node2
        graph->deleteObserversTransactional(node2);
        // Create node3 and add as observer
        auto node3 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
        graph->addNode(node3);
        graph->addObCb(node2, node3);
    }

    // Trigger notification on node2 (second sensor)
    node2->valueChanged();

    // Record notification count after second binding
    int afterSecond = node2->notificationCount();

    // Rollback to original state
    graph->rollbackToSavePoint(savePoint);

    // Trigger notification on node1 (first sensor)
    node1->valueChanged();

    // Record notification count after rollback
    int afterRollback = node2->notificationCount();

    return RollbackObservation{afterSecond, afterRollback};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {

    auto expr = reaction::createExpr([=]() { return var->get(); });

    int updatedVar = baseValue + increment;
    
    // Update the variable
    var->set(updatedVar);
    
    // Get updated values
    int updatedExpr = expr->get();
    int squared = calc->get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExpr,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExpr,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}

const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const noexcept { return m_name; }

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto a = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("A");
    auto b = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("B");
    auto c = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("C");

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(a);
    graph.registerNode(b);
    graph.registerNode(c);

    graph.addObserver(a, b, true);   // Direct
    graph.addObserver(b, c, true);   // Direct (transitive via B)

    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    graph.collectObservers(a, observersSet);

    ObserverCollectionSummary summary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = false,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = false
    };

    for (const auto& wp : observersSet) {
        if (auto node = wp.lock()) {
            if (node->name() == "B") [[likely]]
                summary.containsDirectObserver = true;
            if (node->name() == "C") [[likely]]
                summary.containsTransitiveObserver = true;
        }
    }

    return summary;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto value = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(baseValue);
    auto recomputeCount = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(0);

    // Create a calculation that increments recomputeCount whenever value changes
    auto calc = std::make_shared<CalcExpr<int>>([=](int val) {
        (*recomputeCount)++;
        return 0; // Dummy return value
    }, value);

    // Create and execute the batch operations
    Batch batch;
    batch.addOp([deltaA = deltaA](const NodePtr &node) -> bool {
        auto ptr = node->getPtr<int>();
        if (!ptr) [[unlikely]] {
            return false;
        }
        *ptr += deltaA;
        return true;
    });
    batch.addOp([deltaB = deltaB](const NodePtr &node) -> bool {
        auto ptr = node->getPtr<int>();
        if (!ptr) [[unlikely]] {
            return false;
        }
        *ptr += deltaB;
        return true;
    });

    int recomputeCountBefore = *recomputeCount;
    batch.execute();
    int recomputeCountAfter = *recomputeCount;
    int finalValue = *value;

    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCountBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCountAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;
};

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> reactiveVar(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Create variable to track recomputation count
    reaction::Var<int> recomputeCount(0);
    
    // Step 3: Create calculation that increments the recompute count
    auto calc = reaction::calc([this](const int& v) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return v; // Dummy return, actual value isn't used
    }, reactiveVar);
    
    // Step 4: Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch;
    batch.add(calc);
    
    // Step 5: Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // Step 6: Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // Step 7: Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount.get();
    
    // Step 8: Close batch
    batch.close();
    
    // Step 9: Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // Step 10: Ensure calculation was evaluated (recompute count should be at least 1)
    // Note: Depending on the reaction framework's behavior, this might be 1 or 2.
    // Here, assuming the calculation is triggered once during batch execution.
    
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        .closedInitially = closedInitially,
        .closedAfterClose = closedAfterClose,
        .recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // 4. Get thread count
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 10. Return transition
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = before_enable,
        .afterEnable = after_enable,
        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    auto mutableVar = reaction::VarExpr::create(baseValue);
    auto constVar = reaction::ConstExpr::create(constValue);
    auto sumCalc = reaction::CalcExpr::create(reaction::AddOp{mutableVar, constVar});
    int initialSum = sumCalc->getValue();
    mutableVar->set(baseValue + delta);
    int updatedSum = sumCalc->getValue();
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

template <typename Expr, typename Type>
using VarExpr = reaction::VarExpr<Expr, Type>;

template <typename Op, typename L, typename R>
using CalcExpr = reaction::CalcExpr<Op, L, R>;

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    VarExpr<reaction::ValueOp, int> var(initialValue);
    VarExpr<reaction::ValueOp, int> counter(0);
    CalcExpr<reaction::MultiplyOp, VarExpr<reaction::ValueOp, int>, VarExpr<reaction::ValueOp, int>> calc(
        [this](const int& v) {
            counter.set(counter.get() + 1);
            return v * 2;
        },
        var,
        reaction::AlwaysTrig()
    );

    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter.get();
    var.set(initialValue);
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter.get();
    int dummy = calc.get();
    int dummyCounter = counter.get();

    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = recomputeCountAfterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([&]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g([this](const std::string& name) {});
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBeforeGuard,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfterGuard,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBeforeGuard,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable with the initial value
    auto var = std::make_shared<VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that uses the variable with LastHandle policy
    auto calc = std::make_shared<CalcExpr>(OpExpr::IdentityOp(), var, LastHandle());
    
    // Get the initial result
    int initialResult = calc->getValue();
    
    // Update the variable's value
    var->update(updatedValue);
    
    // Get the result after updating
    int updatedResult = calc->getValue();
    
    // Close the source variable node
    var->close();
    
    // Get the result after the source is closed
    int closedResult = calc->getValue();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = closedResult
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitor& sm = StackMonitor::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    sm.disable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    sm.initialize();
    
    StackMonitorReport report;
    
    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        StackGuard<sizeof(StackGuard)> guard;
        // 6. Check if guard was successfully allocated
        report.guardAllocated = guard.success();
        
        if (report.guardAllocated) {
            // 7. Get current usage during guard
            report.usageDuringGuard = sm.getCurrentUsage();
            
            // 8. Get statistics
            StackMonitorStats stats = sm.getStats();
            report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(stats.used) / stats.total) * 100.0;
            report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.nearLimit;
        } else {
            report.usageDuringGuard = 0;
            report.usagePercentage = 0.0;
            report.nearLimitDuringGuard = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = sm.getCurrentUsage();
    
    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    sm.disable();
    
    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <functional>
#include "reaction/core/id_generator.h"
#include "reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h"

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Step 1: Create lambda function to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed](const void*) {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + static_cast<int>(i);
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Step 2: Create small SBOResource with initial value
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes{initialSmall};

    // Step 3: Create large SBOResource using the lambda
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes{createLargePayload};

    // Step 4: Update small resource's value
    auto updateResult = smallRes.update(updatedSmall);
    bool updateReportedChange = updateResult.changed();

    // Step 5: Get storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeRes.getStorageInfo();

    // Step 6: Compile status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.is_sbo,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stack_usage,
        .largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.is_sbo};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    auto& thread_manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();

    ConditionalMutex mutex;

    {
        ConditionalLockGuard<ConditionalMutex> guard(mutex, false);
        // Lock without safety, should succeed

    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    {
        ConditionalLockGuard<ConditionalMutex> guard(mutex, true);
        // Lock with safety, should succeed
    }

    };
}

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_shared_mutex.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety{false};
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety{false};
    bool trySharedWithSafety{false};
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety{false};
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    SharedMutexResult result;
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    
    // Test without safety
    result.trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.trySharedNoSafety();
    if (result.trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlockShared();
    }
    
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.tryUniqueNoSafety();
        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        }
    }
    
    // Enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Test with safety
    try {
        mutex.tryShared();
        result.trySharedWithSafety = true;
        mutex.unlockShared();
    } catch (...) {
        result.trySharedWithSafety = false;
    }
    
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLock<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> lock(mutex);
        try {
            mutex.tryUnique();
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = true;
            mutex.unlockUnique();
        } catch (...) {
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = false;
        }
    }
    
    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    for (const auto& code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::getErrorDescription(code));
    }
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    reaction::ReactionException ex(message, code, file, line, function);
    ExceptionInfo info{
        .formattedMessage = ex.getFormattedMessage(),
        .originalMessage = ex.getOriginalMessage(),
        .file = ex.getFile(),
        .line = ex.getLine(),
        .function = ex.getFunction(),
        .codeName = ex.getCodeName()
    };
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    bool stable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initial,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = newValue,
        .pointerStable = stable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <shared_mutex>

namespace test_reaction {

struct Counter {
    int value{0};
    void add(int delta) noexcept { value += delta; }
};

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    auto node = std::make_shared<reaction::Node>();
    reaction::VarExpr<Counter> counterVar = reaction::VarExpr<Counter>::create(*node, Counter{initialValue});
    
    auto ptr1 = counterVar.getPtr();
    ptr1->add(delta);
    int val1 = ptr1->value;
    
    auto ptr2 = counterVar.getPtr();
    ptr2->add(delta);
    int val2 = ptr2->value;
    
    bool stable = ptr1.lock() == ptr2.lock();
    
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = val1,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = val2,
        .pointerStable = stable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables
    React::Var var1(firstName);
    React::Var var2(secondName);
    React::Var var3(thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them
    std::set<React::Var> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);

    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& var : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(var.getName());
    }

    // Check if all names are unique
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = (uniqueNames.size() == orderedNames.size());

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)), m_notificationCount(0) {}
    int notifications() const { return m_notificationCount; }
    void clear() { m_notificationCount = 0; }

private:
    std::atomic<int> m_notificationCount;
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName,
                                      const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_unique<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_unique<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(source->shared_from_this());
    graph.registerNode(target->shared_from_this());
    graph.addObserver(source->shared_from_this(), target->shared_from_this());

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedBefore = target->notifications() > 0;

    target->clear();

    target->reset();

    source->notify();

    bool notifiedAfter = target->notifications() > 0;

    source->close();
    target->close();

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>  // Assuming StackMonitor and StackGuard are here
#include <memory>
#include <new>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackAllocationSnapshot {
    bool smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool runtimeAllowedWithoutMonitoring;
    bool largeAllowedWithMonitoring;
    bool guardSucceeded;
    bool allocationBlockedAtLimit;
    bool runtimeAllowedAfterRelease;
};

inline StackAllocationSnapshot test_stack_monitor_capacity(std::size_t largeSize) {
    StackAllocationSnapshot snapshot;

    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // Step 3: Check small allocation without monitoring
    const size_t small_size = 1024; // Example small size
    snapshot.smallAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(small_size);

    // Step 4: Check large allocation without monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithoutMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 5: Enable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();

    // Step 6: Re-initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().init();

    // Step 7: Check large allocation with monitoring
    snapshot.largeAllowedWithMonitoring = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 8: Create StackGuard in scope
    bool guard_succeeded = false;
    try {
        StackGuard g(largeSize);
        guard_succeeded = true;
    } catch (...) {
        // Guard creation failed, treat as not succeeded
        guard_succeeded = false;
    }
    snapshot.guardSucceeded = guard_succeeded;

    // Step 9: Check if allocation is blocked at limit
    snapshot.allocationBlockedAtLimit = !StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    // Step 10: Exit scope (guard is automatically destroyed)

    // Step 11: Check runtime allocation after release
    snapshot.runtimeAllowedAfterRelease = StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocate(largeSize);

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**
1. **Disable Monitoring:** `StackMonitor::getInstance().disable()` turns off stack usage tracking.
2. **Initialize Monitor:** `init()` resets stack usage counters.
3. **Check Allocations Without Monitoring:** Uses `canAllocate` to verify small and large allocations when monitoring is disabled.
4. **Enable Monitoring:** `StackMonitor::getInstance().enable()` activates stack tracking.
5. **Re-Initialize Monitor:** Ensures stack usage is reset after enabling monitoring.
6. **Check With Monitoring:** Verifies large allocation behavior under monitoring.
7. **StackGuard Creation:** Attempts to create a `StackGuard` with `largeSize`, catching exceptions to determine success.
8. **Allocation Block Check:** Confirms that subsequent allocations are blocked due to the guard's usage.
9. **Post-Guard Check:** Validates that allocations succeed after the guard is destroyed.

This implementation follows the problem's steps, leveraging the `reaction` library's stack monitoring and guard mechanisms to test allocation behaviors under different conditions.
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#include <memory>
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/field_graph.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { ++m_notifications; }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    mutable int m_notifications{0}; ///< Counter for value change notifications.
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field nodes
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Field");

    // Step 2: Get graphs
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Register nodes
    observerGraph->addNode(owner);
    observerGraph->addNode(field);

    // Step 4: Register field under object ID
    fieldGraph->addObjField(objectId, field);

    // Step 5: Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph->bindField(objectId, "Field", owner);

    // Step 6: Trigger value change on field
    field->valueChanged();

    // Step 7: Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = owner->notifications() > 0;

    // Step 8: Clear owner's notification count
    owner->clear();

    // Step 9: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph->closeNode(owner);

    // Step 10: Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph->deleteObjField(objectId);

    // Step 11: Attempt to bind field again
    fieldGraph->bindField(objectId, "Field", owner);

    // Step 12: Trigger value change again
    field->valueChanged();

    // Step 13: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = owner->notifications() > 0;

    // Step 14: Close nodes
    observerGraph->closeNode(owner);
    observerGraph->closeNode(field);

    // Step 15: Return result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>
#include <memory.h> // for sizeof
#include <new>      // for exception handling

namespace test_reaction {

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor& sm = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance();
    sm.enable();
    sm.init();

    auto statsBefore = sm.getUsageStats();

    // Check if template-based allocation is allowed
    bool templateAllowed = false;
    try {
        struct LargeType { char buffer[1024]; }; // Template type with fixed size
        reaction::memory::StackGuard<LargeType> g;
        templateAllowed = true;
    } catch (...) {
        templateAllowed = false;
    }

    // Check if runtime-based allocation is allowed
    bool runtimeAllowed = false;
    try {
        reaction::memory::StackGuard<sizeof(int) * 1000> g; // Runtime size allocation
        runtimeAllowed = true;
    } catch (...) {
        runtimeAllowed = false;
    }

    // Create a large StackGuard to exceed stack limits
    bool guardHeld = false;
    try {
        reaction::memory::StackGuard<sizeof(int) * 10000> g; // Large allocation
        guardHeld = true;
    } catch (...) {
        guardHeld = false;
    }

    // Check if allocation is blocked while the guard is active
    bool allocationBlocked = false;
    if (guardHeld) {
        try {
            reaction::memory::StackGuard<sizeof(int) * 100> g; // Small allocation attempt
        } catch (...) {
            allocationBlocked = true;
        }
    }

    auto statsAfter = sm.getUsageStats();
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBefore,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfter,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<reaction::Void>>();
    reaction::Void value = resource->getValue();
    bool usesSboStorage = resource->uses_sbo();
    bool retrievedIsVoid = sizeof(value) == 0;

    reaction::ObserverGraph &graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto nodePtr = resource->shared_from_this();
    graph.registerNode(nodePtr);
    graph.setName(nodePtr, nodeName);
    bool nameAssigned = graph.getName(nodePtr) == nodeName;

    graph.closeNode(nodePtr);
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = graph.getName(nodePtr).empty();

    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    CountingObserver co;
    reaction::ObserverNode src1, src2, src3;
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(&src1);
    graph.registerNode(&src2);
    graph.registerNode(&src3);
    
    src1.addObserver(&co);
    src1.valueChanged();
    int first = co.notifications();
    
    src1.removeObserver(&co);
    src2.addObserver(&co);
    src3.addObserver(&co);
    
    src1.valueChanged();
    int firstAfter = co.notifications();
    
    src2.valueChanged();
    int secondAfter = co.notifications();
    
    src3.valueChanged();
    int thirdAfter = co.notifications();
    
    graph.closeAllNodes();
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = first,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfter,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = secondAfter,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = thirdAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> var(5);

    // Step 2: Create counter to track calculation evaluations
    reaction::Var<int> counter(0);

    // Step 3: Create calculation that triggers counter increment on evaluation
    auto calc = reaction::CalcExpr([this](const int& val) {
        counter++;
        return val;
    }, var);

    // Step 4: Apply atomic addition (+= 3)
    var.atomicAddAssign(3);
    int afterAddition = var.get();

    // Step 5: Apply atomic multiplication (*= 2)
    var.atomicMultiplyAssign(2);
    int afterMultiplication = var.get();

    // Step 6: Apply atomic bitwise AND (&= 0xF)
    var.atomicBitwiseAndAssign(0xF);
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var.get();

    // Step 7: Apply atomic left shift (<<= 1)
    var.atomicLeftShiftAssign(1);
    int afterShift = var.get();

    // Step 8: Apply post-increment (++var)
    int postIncrementReturn = var.postIncrement();
    int finalValue = var.get();

    // Step 9: Return the snapshot
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAddition,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMultiplication,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterBitwiseAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postIncrementReturn,
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .calcEvaluations = counter.get()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthProbeNode : public ObserverNode {
    int depth;
    DepthProbeNode() : depth(0) {}
    int getDepth() const { return depth; }
    void updateDepth(int d) { depth = d; }
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    auto& thread_manager = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();

    auto shallow = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    shallow->depth = 1;
    auto deep = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    deep->depth = 2;

    NodeWeak shallow_weak = shallow;
    NodeWeak deep_weak = deep;

    BatchCompare<ObserverNode> batch_compare;

    bool initialResult = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    shallow->updateDepth(3);
    deep->updateDepth(2);
    bool shallowBeforeDeep = batch_compare.compare(shallow_weak, deep_weak);

    shallow->updateDepth(1);
    deep->updateDepth(3);
    bool deepBeforeShallow = batch_compare.compare(deep_weak, shallow_weak);

    NodeWeak null_weak = nullptr;
    bool nullComesFirst = batch_compare.compare(null_weak, shallow_weak);

    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeep,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallow,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirst
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto varA = VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = VarExpr::create(b);

    // 2. Build negated sum expression
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_op(varA, varB, OpType::Add);
    auto negSumExpr = make_unary_op(sumExpr, OpType::Neg);
    auto negSumNode = createReactiveNode(negSumExpr);
    int initialNegatedSum = negSumNode->getValue();

    // 3. Create reactive boolean variable
    auto flagVar = VarExpr::create(false);

    // 4. Build logical NOT expression
    auto notFlagExpr = make_unary_op(flagVar, OpType::LogicalNot);
    auto notFlagNode = createReactiveNode(notFlagExpr);

    // 5. Create reactive integer variable for bitwise NOT
    auto cVar = VarExpr::create(5); // Example value
    auto bitNotExpr = make_unary_op(cVar, OpType::BitwiseNot);
    auto bitNotNode = createReactiveNode(bitNotExpr);
    int bitwiseNotValue = bitNotNode->getValue(); // 7. Get initial bitwise NOT value

    // 8. Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA->update(a + 1);
    varB->update(b + 1);
    flagVar->update(true);

    // 9. Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negSumNode->getValue();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip = notFlagNode->getValue();

    // 10. Return snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = logicalNotAfterFlip,
        .bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotValue
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    // Step 1: Define IntCache class inheriting from CacheBase
    class IntCache : public CacheBase {
    public:
        using CacheBase::insert;
        using CacheBase::get;
        using CacheBase::getStats;
        using CacheBase::invalidateAll;
        using CacheBase::triggerCleanup;
    };

    // Step 3: Create IntCache instance
    IntCache cache;

    // Step 4: Get initial cache statistics
    auto initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.totalEntries;

    // Step 5: Perform initial lookup (expect miss)
    int value;
    auto firstResult = cache.get(42, value);
    bool firstLookupWasMiss = !firstResult.has_value();

    // Step 6: Insert value into cache
    if (firstLookupWasMiss) {
        cache.insert(42, 42);
    }

    // Step 7: Perform second lookup (expect hit)
    auto secondResult = cache.get(42, value);
    bool secondLookupWasHit = secondResult.has_value();

    // Step 8: Get cache stats after insertion
    auto postInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.totalEntries;
    std::size_t hitCount = postInsertStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount = postInsertStats.missCount;

    // Step 9: Invalidate all cache entries
    cache.invalidateAll();

    // Step 10: Trigger cleanup
    cache.triggerCleanup();

    // Step 11: Get stats after invalidation
    auto postInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidationStats.totalEntries;

    // Step 12: Calculate hit ratio
    double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount);

    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterInsert = entriesAfterInsert,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate,
        .hitCount = hitCount,
        .missCount = missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstLookupWasMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondLookupWasHit
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    ForceRegisterSnapshot snapshot{
        .safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled(),
    };

    tm.forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    snapshot.safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([this](const std::function<void()> &f = []() {
        reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().forceRegisterCurrentThread();
    }) {
        std::invoke(f);
    });
    worker.join();

    snapshot.safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the atomic helper wrapper for testing compound assignment operations.
 * Tracks value changes and reports whether each operation modified the stored value.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    /**
     * Generic atomic operation helper that updates the stored value and tracks changes.
     * @tparam F Operation function type (expected to take int32_t and return new int32_t).
     * @param op The operation to perform on the current value.
     */
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        int32_t newValue = op(oldValue);
        m_value = newValue;
        m_lastReportedChange = (newValue != oldValue);
    }

    /**
     * Enhanced atomic operation helper that allows forcing change detection.
     * @tparam F Operation function type.
     * @param op The operation to perform on the current value.
     * @param forceNotify If true, always report a change regardless of actual value difference.
     */
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        int32_t newValue = op(oldValue);
        m_value = newValue;
        m_lastReportedChange = (newValue != oldValue) || forceNotify;
    }

    int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    std::atomic<int32_t> m_value;
    std::atomic<bool> m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Captures the results of atomic compound operations for validation.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Tests compound assignment operations using reaction framework helpers.
 * @param initial Starting value for the atomic counter.
 * @return A snapshot of operation results and final value.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);

    // Apply operations and capture change flags
    bool addChanged = reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 5);
    bool multiplyChanged = reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 2);
    bool andChanged = reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0b111);
    bool leftShiftChanged = reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 1);
    bool incrementChanged = reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic);

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = atomic.value(),
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
